functionality"},{"id":909268321,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw5MDkyNjgzMjE=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/feature","name":"feature","color":"5664ff","default":false,"description":"Indicates new feature / enhancement requests"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":48,"created_at":"2023-08-17T16:57:56Z","updated_at":"2024-11-05T06:37:43Z","closed_at":"2023-09-16T12:51:15Z","author_association":"MEMBER","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/pulls/50958","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/50958","diff_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/50958.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/50958.patch","merged_at":"2023-09-16T12:51:15Z"},"body":"Alternative proposal to #35833, based on a discussion with @gbaraldi.

ScopedVariables are containers whose observed value depends the current
dynamic scope. This implementation is inspired by https://openjdk.org/jeps/446

A scope is introduced with the `scoped` function that takes a lambda to
execute within the new scope. The value of a `ScopedValue` is
constant within that scope and can only be set upon introduction
of a new scope.

Scopes are propagated across tasks boundaries.

In contrast to #35833 the storage of the per-scope data does not require copies 
upon scope entry. This also means that libraries can use scoped variables without
paying for scoped variables introduces in other libraries.

Finding the current value of a `ScopedValue`, involves walking the
scope chain upwards and checking if the scoped variable has a value
for the current or one of its parent scopes. This means the cost of
a lookup scales with the depth of the dynamic scoping. This is amortized
by perform some caching.

https://github.com/vchuravy/ScopedValues.jl provides an implementation
reusing the logstate trick from `ContextVariablesX.jl`. We could use it
as an implementation from Julia 1.7+ on-wards.

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226","id":2122185953,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5-ffzh","number":53226,"title":"Waiting multiple tasks simultaneously","user":{"login":"mrkn","id":3959,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM5NTk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/3959?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn","html_url":"https://github.com/mrkn","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mrkn/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":184008807,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxODQwMDg4MDc=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/domain:multithreading","name":"domain:multithreading","color":"fbca04","default":false,"description":"Base.Threads and related functionality"}],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-02-07T04:55:03Z","updated_at":"2024-06-25T06:31:39Z","closed_at":"2024-02-15T08:22:35Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I wrote two methods that can wait for multiple tasks like Python’s `asyncio.wait`.

The two methods, `waitany` and `waitall`, have the following features:

* The `waitany` function waits at least one of the given tasks.
* The `waitall` function normally waits for all the given tasks. This function has an optional keyword argument `failfast`. If `failfast` is `true`, the function returns at least one of the given tasks stops by exception.
* Both functions return a 2-tuple of vectors. The first vector consists of finished tasks. The second vector consists of tasks that have not been done at the time of finish detection.

The implementation and tests for `waitany` and `waitall` methods are hosted in my repository.  You can find the implementation of these methods [here](https://github.com/mrkn/julia/blob/ae8b0cbfc05bec54da2a433afd7b0c6deb5bd792/base/task.jl#L368-L400).  The associated tests are available [here](https://github.com/mrkn/julia/blob/ae8b0cbfc05bec54da2a433afd7b0c6deb5bd792/test/threads_exec.jl#L1190-L1260).

These methods can be provided in Julia’s base library because the features should have common needs, IMO. I want to submit a pull request to add these two methods, but I have some questions about these methods.

## Questions

1. `waitany` and `waitall` can be combined into one method like `asyncio.wait` but I have chosen not to do so. The reason is that I think `waitany` and `waitall` are more readable and clearer than `asyncio.wait` because these names explicitly convey their functionality, making it easier for developers to understand they wait multiple tasks without checking the arguments. I want to know which interface is more suitable for Julia’s standard library design convention.
2. Julia has other waitable objects than a task, such as a channel and an IO. I think we can make `waitall` and `waitany` be able to wait for all kinds of waitables. Which task-specific or more universal is suitable for Julia’s standard feature?
3. Both functions return a 2-tuple of `Vector{Task}` whereas `asyncio.wait` returns a 2-tuple of sets of tasks.  Which return type is better?
4. It is my first challenge of working on Julia’s core. If there are any other considerations, I want to know them.
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226/reactions","total_count":4,"+1":4,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2188087070","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226#issuecomment-2188087070","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53226","id":2188087070,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Ca48e","user":{"login":"mind6","id":9737618,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk3Mzc2MTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9737618?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6","html_url":"https://github.com/mind6","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-25T06:31:38Z","updated_at":"2024-06-25T06:31:38Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> `throwto` may cause problems where the program crashes, so is fairly strongly discouraged. A `jl_cpu_pause` call will not help with fixing the design problem there. Take a look at the implementation of `Experimental.@sync` if you want to see how to implement a `waitany`/`waitall` call correctly (it implements waitall with failfast).

Can you explain more about the issue with `throwto`? It sounds like the choice is between this or task leaking. If `throwto` is acceptable then we're saying tasks that hang on `wait(...)` can be reliably terminated. If not acceptable then tasks leaks are responsibility of user; she must choose to close the Channel at some point, or to reset the Event object.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2188087070/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217","id":635798799,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU2MzU3OTg3OTk=","number":36217,"title":"`wait()` with timeout","user":{"login":"staticfloat","id":130920,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzMDkyMA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/130920?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat","html_url":"https://github.com/staticfloat","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/staticfloat/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":9159719,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw5MTU5NzE5","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/domain:parallelism","name":"domain:parallelism","color":"f7e102","default":false,"description":"Parallel or distributed computation"},{"id":688843543,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWw2ODg4NDM1NDM=","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/labels/status:triage","name":"status:triage","color":"e4003c","default":false,"description":"This should be discussed on a triage call"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":61,"created_at":"2020-06-09T22:28:29Z","updated_at":"2024-06-25T07:34:53Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"It appears that we do not support timeouts in `wait()`.  To motivate the need for this, it's a pretty critical basic functionality for writing resilient servers; blocking forever due to a dropped packet is unacceptable, so servers must instead be written in an explicitly polling style, which is bad for latency and for CPU usage.

Implementation-wise, `wait(c)` waits upon a condition for a `notify()` to push it back onto the scheduler's work queue.  It seems to me that timeouts can be implemented by starting a second task that `sleep()`'s for a certain amount of time, then unconditionally `notify()`'s that condition.  The consumer of the `wait()` call would then need to disambiguate a timeout from an actual event.

Taking a look at the different things that can be `wait()`'ed upon, most are implemented in terms of `Condition` objects, so a very simple `@async (sleep(timeout); notify(c))` should work, and the `_FDWatcher` from `FileWatching` also notifies a condition in the end, therefore I believe a uniform API is possible here.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217/reactions","total_count":20,"+1":19,"-1":1,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2188190284","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217#issuecomment-2188190284","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/36217","id":2188190284,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6CbSJM","user":{"login":"mind6","id":9737618,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk3Mzc2MTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9737618?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6","html_url":"https://github.com/mind6","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-25T07:34:51Z","updated_at":"2024-06-25T07:34:51Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> Julia now has `waitall` and `waitany` for tasks so I imagine this work would be an expansion of that work to be able to wait on a timer.

If you're using `waitany` to wait for a condition plus a Timer, it still requires a separate task to wait on the condition, and it will not do anything to "unleak" the task for you.

Fundamentally if you cannot interrupt tasks safely, you have to release the object it's waiting on to end the task. The user just has to manage the waitable object to prevent leaks. As previous posts have said, `wait_timeout` should not assume the object should be closed or notified or what not. It should only guarantee releasing the waitable object later will not cause side-affects; the waiter task it creates will terminate immediately and make no state changes. ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2188190284/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

rl":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/milestones/44/labels","id":7729972,"node_id":"MI_kwDOABkWpM4AdfM0","number":44,"title":"1.9","description":"","creator":{"login":"vtjnash","id":330950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjMzMDk1MA==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/330950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash","html_url":"https://github.com/vtjnash","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/vtjnash/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":20,"closed_issues":41,"state":"open","created_at":"2022-03-02T19:34:37Z","updated_at":"2022-11-03T10:46:09Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":8,"created_at":"2022-09-05T10:30:27Z","updated_at":"2022-11-05T07:51:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Since Julia 1.8, a single Ctrl-C does not work, I always need to repeat Ctrl-C multiple times to get the forced SIGINT (with `WARNING: Force throwing a SIGINT`):


https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6933510/188428251-dec8a3da-7410-4ebf-b001-8de77457e14b.mov

I found this to be the case for anything that I interrupt, not just `sleep`. This means that, to exit Pluto, Jupyter, a web server or any other long running process, users will now need to **hold down** Ctrl-C, [printing multiple WARNINGs](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6933510/188687702-daf3e8f4-7bbc-447e-afde-7c9c03caca32.png), which makes Julia look unstable.

```
Julia Version 1.8.0
Commit 5544a0fab76 (2022-08-17 13:38 UTC)
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873","id":403917189,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWU0MDM5MTcxODk=","number":30873,"title":"module __init__() is called multiple times","user":{"login":"orenbenmeir","id":42718179,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQyNzE4MTc5","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42718179?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir","html_url":"https://github.com/orenbenmeir","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/orenbenmeir/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":8,"created_at":"2019-01-28T17:10:33Z","updated_at":"2022-11-10T16:20:09Z","closed_at":"2019-01-28T22:34:55Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"In the documentation it is the __init__() function will be called only one time in the lifecycle of a module. I've cases were it is being called multiple times.

```
# a.jl
module a
function __init__()
	println("$(@__MODULE__)::__init")
end
end

# b.jl
module b
function __init__()
	println("$(@__MODULE__)::__init")
end
end

# c.jl
module c
import a
import b
end

# d.jl
module d
import c
import b
end


julia> using d
[ Info: Precompiling d [top-level]
a::__init
b::__init
b::__init
a::__init
b::__init
a::__init
```
This will happen only the first time when the module is not precompiled. Consecutive calls will call __init() only once for each module.
I've a case were the pre-compilation is broken due to dependencies with old packages and it is causing the multiple invocations cause other problems.
I've tested it both with Julia 1.0.3 and with version 1.1
","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1310546244","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873#issuecomment-1310546244","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/30873","id":1310546244,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5OHVlE","user":{"login":"mind6","id":9737618,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk3Mzc2MTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9737618?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6","html_url":"https://github.com/mind6","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-11-10T16:20:08Z","updated_at":"2022-11-10T16:20:08Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"It would be nice to have a verbose print out option of module loading and initialization so people aren't puzzled by this. {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989","id":1300379328,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5NgjbA","number":45989,"title":"presence of unreachable code causes macro created variables to become undefined","user":{"login":"mind6","id":9737618,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk3Mzc2MTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9737618?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6","html_url":"https://github.com/mind6","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-07-11T08:28:15Z","updated_at":"2022-07-11T08:28:15Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I found an issue that may not be serious for most users, but perhaps it's pointing to compiler behavior that can result in more serious problems.  The error is reproducible in versions 1.7.3 and 1.8RC1.

This is the minimal example I made.

```julia
module A
    macro define(var)
        quote
            local sym = Symbol($var)
            $(__module__).eval(:($sym = 1234))
        end
    end
end

module B
    import ..A
    function run_succeed()
        A.@define "x"
        println("x in B is: ", x)
    end

    function run_succeed2()
        if false x = 6789 end
        A.@define "x"
        println("B.x is: ", B.x)
    end

    function run_fail()
        if false x = 6789 end
        A.@define "x"
        println("x in B is now: ", x)
    end
end
# versioninfo()
B.run_succeed()
B.run_succeed2()
B.run_fail()
```
`A.@define` takes a String, and defines a variable of that name in the caller's global module scope. This is the output I get:

```
x in B is: 1234
B.x is: 1234
ERROR: UndefVarError: x not defined
Stacktrace:
 [1] run_fail()
   @ Main.B ellided...\bug_varundef.jl:26
 [2] top-level scope
   @ ellided...\bug_varundef.jl:32
```

The presence of the line `if false x = 6789 end` caused `x` to be undefined when accessing it without the module qualifier. But access is okay when qualified as `B.x`.

The reason I have the unreachable code is to help the vscode linter avoid warnings. There are simple workarounds for this issue, but I wonder why the compiler is being influenced by the presence of unreachable code? ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

found an issue that may not be serious for most users, but perhaps it's pointing to compiler behavior that can result in more serious problems.  The error is reproducible in versions 1.7.3 and 1.8RC1.

This is the minimal example I made.

```julia
module A
    macro define(var)
        quote
            local sym = Symbol($var)
            $(__module__).eval(:($sym = 1234))
        end
    end
end

module B
    import ..A
    function run_succeed()
        A.@define "x"
        println("x in B is: ", x)
    end

    function run_succeed2()
        if false x = 6789 end
        A.@define "x"
        println("B.x is: ", B.x)
    end

    function run_fail()
        if false x = 6789 end
        A.@define "x"
        println("x in B is now: ", x)
    end
end
# versioninfo()
B.run_succeed()
B.run_succeed2()
B.run_fail()
```
`A.@define` takes a String, and defines a variable of that name in the caller's global module scope. This is the output I get:

```
x in B is: 1234
B.x is: 1234
ERROR: UndefVarError: x not defined
Stacktrace:
 [1] run_fail()
   @ Main.B ellided...\bug_varundef.jl:26
 [2] top-level scope
   @ ellided...\bug_varundef.jl:32
```

The presence of the line `if false x = 6789 end` caused `x` to be undefined when accessing it without the module qualifier. But access is okay when qualified as `B.x`.

The reason I have the unreachable code is to help the vscode linter avoid warnings. There are simple workarounds for this issue, but I wonder why the compiler is being influenced by the presence of unreachable code? ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"not_planned"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1180394671","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989#issuecomment-1180394671","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/45989","id":1180394671,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM5GW2Sv","user":{"login":"mind6","id":9737618,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjk3Mzc2MTg=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/9737618?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6","html_url":"https://github.com/mind6","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/mind6/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2022-07-11T13:13:53Z","updated_at":"2022-07-11T13:13:53Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> That is simply how normal eval and scope rules works, and not anything to do with macros. You could use a function instead of a macro there, and your program would be much clearer

But `B.run_succeed()` works. The only difference is the one unreachable line of code from `B.run_fail()`. You can try to use differently names variables for each function, and see the same behavior.  Having `if false x = 6789 end` either before or after the `A.@define` call, causes x to be invisible.
